title: Sustainable, Resilient, and Just Global Clean Energy Supply Chains
text:
Achieving carbon neutrality requires deploying renewable energy at unprecedented speed and scale, yet countries sometimes implement decoupling policies that might impede continuous cost declining. International climate policy and renewable energy deployment policy now face a crossroads: continue relying on global supply chains, or pivot towards domestic technology development and production.
This project will evaluate the carbon mitigation, air quality, and human health benefits achieved by global clean energy supply chains, using solar PV, a globally trade clean energy product, as an example. This project will be one of a series of evidence-based research and analysis to show the importance to maintain a sustainable and resilient clean energy global supply chains to achieve climate goals.

title: Deep Policy Lab
text:
Data driven


Evidence based


Energy and climate


Policy insights for energy decisions and climate actions

title: About Deep Policy Lab
text: The Deep Energy and Climate Policy Lab (Deep Policy Lab) provides data-driven, evidence based, enabled by big data and AI, energy and climate policy analysis and research to support energy decisions and climate actions. Deep Policy Lab aims to advance actionable and evidence-based energy and climate solutions through data-driven analysis and dialogue. Deep Policy Lab leverages big data, machine learning, and systems modeling to inform policy decisions on renewable energy, clean energy supply chains, land use changes, deep decarbonization, and other topics related to the energy transition and carbon neutrality. Deep Policy Lab collaborates with academic institutions, government agencies, industry partners, and civil society organizations to foster innovation and impact in the field of energy and climate policy.
